Adaptability.effect: Powers up moves of the same type as the Pokémon.

Aerilate.effect: Normal-type moves become Flying-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.

Aftermath.effect: Damages the attacker if it contacts the Pokémon with a finishing hit.

Analytic.effect: Boosts move power when the Pokémon moves last.

Anger Point.effect: The Pokémon is angered when it takes a critical hit, and that maxes its Attack stat.

Anticipation.effect: The Pokémon can sense an opposing Pokémon's dangerous moves.

Armor Tail.effect: The mysterious tail covering the Pokémon's head makes opponents unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.

Aroma Veil.effect: Protects itself and its allies from attacks that limit their move choices.

Battle Bond.effect: Defeating an opposing Pokémon strengthens the Pokémon's bond with its Trainer, and it becomes Ash-Greninja. Water Shuriken gets more powerful.In Scarlet & Violet, changes to boost Attack, Speed and Special Attack when opponents are knocked out.

Berserk.effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat when it takes a  hit that causes its HP to become half or less.

Big Pecks.effect: Protects the Pokémon from Defense-lowering effects.

Blaze.effect: Powers up Fire-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

Bulletproof.effect: Protects the Pokémon from some ball and bomb moves.

Cheek Pouch.effect: Restores HP as well when the Pokémon eats a Berry.

Chlorophyll.effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in sunshine.

Clear Body.effect: Prevents other Pokémon's moves or Abilities from lowering the Pokémon's stats.

Competitive.effect: Boosts the Sp. Atk stat sharply when a stat is lowered.

Compoundeyes.effect: The Pokémon's compound eyes boost its accuracy.

Contrary.effect: Makes stat changes have an opposite effect.

Corrosion.effect: The Pokémon can poison the target even if it's a Steel or Poison type.

Cud Chew.effect: When the Pokémon eats a Berry, it will regurgitate that Berry at the end of the next turn and eat it one more time.

Curious Medicine.effect: When the Pokémon  is brought into battle, any stat alterations of allies are removed

Cursed Body.effect: May disable a move used on the Pokémon.

Cute Charm.effect: Contact with the Pokémon may cause infatuation.

Damp.effect: Prevents the use of explosive moves such as Self-Destruct by dampening its surroundings.

Defiant.effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat sharply when its stats are lowered.

Disguise.effect: Once per battle, the shroud that covers the Pokémon can protect it from an attack.

Dragonize.effect: The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Dragon-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.

Drizzle.effect: The Pokémon makes it rain when it enters a battle.

Drought.effect: Turns the sunlight harsh when the Pokémon enters a battle.

Dry Skin.effect: Restores HP in rain or when hit by Water-type moves. Reduces HP in sunshine, and increases the damage received from Fire-type moves.

Early Bird.effect: The Pokémon awakens twice as fast as other Pokémon from sleep.

Earth Eater.effect: If hit by a Ground-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

Electromorphosis.effect: The Pokémon becomes charged when it takes damage, boosting the power of the next Electric-type move the Pokémon uses.

Fairy Aura.effect: Powers up each Pokémon's Fairy-type moves.

Filter.effect: Reduces the power of supereffective attacks taken.
Filter.damageCondition: when hit by a supereffective move

Flame Body.effect: Contact with the Pokémon may burn the attacker.

Flash Fire.effect: Powers up the Pokémon's Fire-type moves if it's hit by one.

Flower Veil.effect: Ally Grass-type Pokémon are protected from status conditions and the lowering of their stats.

Forecast.effect: The Pokémon transforms with the weather to change its type to Water, Fire, or Ice.

Frisk.effect: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

Fur Coat.effect: Halves the damage from physical moves onto this Pokémon.

Gale Wings.effect: Gives priority to Flying-type moves when the  Pokémon's HP is full.

Gluttony.effect: Makes the Pokémon eat a held Berry when its HP drops to half or less, which is sooner than usual.

Gooey.effect: Contact with the Pokémon lowers the attacker's Speed stat.

Guts.effect: It's so gutsy that having a status condition boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat.
Guts.damageCondition: when the Pokemon has a status condition

Harvest.effect: May create another Berry after one is used.

Healer.effect: Sometimes heals an ally's status condition.

Heatproof.effect: The heatproof body of the Pokémon halves the damage from Fire-type moves that hit it.

Hospitality.effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it showers its ally with hospitality, restoring a small amount of the ally's HP.

Hunger Switch.effect: The Pokémon changes its form, alternating between its Full Belly Mode and Hangry Mode after the end of each turn.

Hustle.effect: Boosts the Attack stat, but lowers accuracy.

Hydration.effect: Heals status conditions if it's raining.

Hyper Cutter.effect: The Pokémon's proud of its powerful pincers. They prevent other Pokémon from lowering its Attack stat.

Ice Body.effect: The Pokémon gradually regains HP in a Snowstorm.

Illuminate.effect: Raises the likelihood of meeting wild Pokémon by illuminating the surroundings.

Illusion.effect: Comes out disguised as the Pokémon in the party's last spot.

Immunity.effect: The immune system of the Pokémon prevents it from getting poisoned.

Imposter.effect: The Pokémon transforms itself into the Pokémon it's facing.

Infiltrator.effect: Passes through the opposing Pokémon's barrier, substitute, and the like and strikes.

Innards Out.effect: Damages the attacker landing the finishing hit by the amount equal to its last HP.

Inner Focus.effect: The Pokémon's intensely focused, and that protects the Pokémon from flinching.

Insomnia.effect: The Pokémon is suffering from insomnia and cannot fall asleep.

Intimidate.effect: The Pokémon intimidates opposing Pokémon upon entering battle, lowering their Attack stat.

Justified.effect: Being hit by a Dark-type move boosts the Attack stat of the Pokémon, for justice.

Keen Eye.effect: Keen eyes prevent other Pokémon from lowering this Pokémon's accuracy.

Klutz.effect: The Pokémon can't use any held items.

Leaf Guard.effect: Prevents status conditions in sunny weather.

Levitate.effect: By floating in the air, the Pokémon receives full immunity to all Ground-type moves.

Lightning Rod.effect: The Pokémon draws in all Electric-type moves.  Instead of being hit by Electric-type moves,  it boosts its Sp. Atk.

Limber.effect: Its limber body protects the Pokémon from paralysis.

Liquid Voice.effect: All sound-based moves become Water-type moves.

Long Reach.effect: The Pokémon uses its moves without making contact with the target.

Magic Bounce.effect: Reflects status moves, instead of getting hit by them.

Magic Guard.effect: The Pokémon only takes damage from attacks.

Magician.effect: The Pokémon steals the held item of a Pokémon it hits with a move.

Magma Armor.effect: The Pokémon is covered with hot magma, which prevents the Pokémon from becoming frozen.

Marvel Scale.effect: The Pokémon's marvelous scales boost the Defense stat if it has a status condition.

Mega Launcher.effect: Powers up aura and pulse moves.

Mega Sol.effect: Even when the sunlight has not turned harsh, the Pokémon can use its moves as if the weather were harsh sunlight.

Merciless.effect: The Pokémon's attacks become critical hits if the target is poisoned.

Mimicry.effect: Changes the Pokémon's type depending on the terrain.

Minus.effect: Boosts the Sp. Atk stat of the Pokémon if an ally with the Plus or Minus Ability is also in battle.

Mirror Armor.effect: Bounces back only the stat-lowering effects that the Pokémon receives.

Mold Breaker.effect: Moves can be used on the target regardless of its Abilities.

Moody.effect: Raises one stat sharply and lowers another every turn.

Motor Drive.effect: Boosts its Speed stat if hit by an Electric-type move, instead of taking damage.

Moxie.effect: The Pokémon shows moxie, and that boosts the Attack stat after knocking out any Pokémon.

Multiscale.effect: Reduces the amount of damage the Pokémon takes when its HP is full.
Multiscale.damageCondition: when defender is at full HP

Mummy.effect: Contact with the Pokémon changes the attacker's Ability to Mummy.

Natural Cure.effect: All status conditions heal when the Pokémon switches out.

No Guard.effect: The Pokémon employs no-guard tactics to ensure incoming and outgoing attacks always land.

Oblivious.effect: The Pokémon is oblivious, and that keeps it from being infatuated or falling for taunts.

Opportunist.effect: If an opponent's stat is boosted, the Pokémon seizes the opportunity to boost the same stat for itself.

Overcoat.effect: Protects the Pokémon from things like sand, hail, and powder.

Overgrow.effect: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

Own Tempo.effect: This Pokémon has its own tempo, and that prevents it from becoming confused.

Pickpocket.effect: Steals an item from an attacker that made direct contact.

Pickup.effect: The Pokémon may pick up the item an opposing Pokémon used during a battle. It may pick up items outside of battle, too.

Piercing Drill.effect: When the Pokémon uses contact moves, it can hit even targets that are protecting themselves, dealing 1/4 of the damage that the move would otherwise deal. Everything aside from the target's protective effects is still triggered.

Pixilate.effect: Normal-type moves become Fairy-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.

Plus.effect: Boosts the Sp. Atk stat of the Pokémon if an ally with the Plus or Minus Ability is also in battle.

Poison Heal.effect: Restores HP if the Pokémon is poisoned, instead of losing HP.

Poison Point.effect: Contact with the Pokémon may poison the attacker.

Poison Touch.effect: May poison a target when the Pokémon makes contact.

Prankster.effect: Gives priority to a status move.

Pressure.effect: By putting pressure on the opposing Pokémon, it raises their PP usage.

Protean.effect: Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use.

Pure Power.effect: Using its pure power, the Pokémon doubles its Attack stat.

Purifying Salt.effect: The Pokémon's pure salt protects it from status conditions and halves the damage taken from Ghost-type moves.

Queenly Majesty.effect: Its majesty pressures the opposing Pokémon, making it unable to attack using priority moves.

Quick Draw.effect: Enables the Pokémon to move first occasionally.

Quick Feet.effect: Boosts the Speed stat if the Pokémon has a status condition.

Rain Dish.effect: The Pokémon gradually regains HP in rain.

Receiver.effect: The Pokémon copies the Ability of a defeated ally.

Reckless.effect: Powers up moves that have recoil damage.

Refrigerate.effect: Normal-type moves become Ice-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.

Regenerator.effect: Restores a little HP when withdrawn from battle.

Ripen.effect: Ripens Berries and doubles their effect.

Rivalry.effect: Becomes competitive and deals more damage to Pokémon of the same gender, but deals less to Pokémon of the opposite gender.

Rock Head.effect: Protects the Pokémon from recoil damage.

Rough Skin.effect: This Pokémon inflicts damage with its rough skin to the attacker on contact.

Sand Force.effect: Boosts the power of Rock-, Ground-, and Steel-type moves in a sandstorm.
Sand Force.damageCondition: Rock-, Ground-, and Steel-type moves in Sandstorm

Sand Rush.effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in a sandstorm.

Sand Spit.effect: The Pokémon creates a sandstorm when it's hit by an attack.

Sand Stream.effect: The Pokémon summons a sandstorm when it enters a battle.

Sand Veil.effect: Boosts the Pokémon's evasion in a sandstorm.

Sap Sipper.effect: Boosts the Attack stat if hit by a Grass-type move, instead of taking damage.

Scrappy.effect: The Pokémon can hit Ghost-type Pokémon with Normal- and Fighting-type moves.

Screen Cleaner.effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, the effects of Light Screen, Reflect, and Aurora Veil are nullified for both opposing and ally Pokémon.

Shadow Tag.effect: This Pokémon steps on the opposing Pokémon's shadow to prevent it from escaping.

Shed Skin.effect: The Pokémon may heal its own status conditions by shedding its skin.

Sheer Force.effect: Removes additional effects to increase the power of moves when attacking.
Sheer Force.damageCondition: moves with secondary effects (secondary effect is removed)

Shell Armor.effect: A hard shell protects the Pokémon from critical hits.

Shield Dust.effect: This Pokémon's dust blocks the additional effects of attacks taken.

Skill Link.effect: Maximizes the number of times multi-strike moves hit.

Slush Rush.effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in a Snowstorm.

Sniper.effect: Powers up moves if they become critical hits when attacking.

Snow Warning.effect: The Pokémon summons a Snowstorm when it enters a battle.

Solar Power.effect: Boosts the Sp. Atk stat in sunny weather, but HP decreases every turn.

Solid Rock.effect: Reduces the power of supereffective attacks taken.
Solid Rock.damageCondition: when hit by a supereffective move

Soundproof.effect: Soundproofing of the Pokémon itself gives full  immunity to all sound-based moves.

Speed Boost.effect: Its Speed stat is boosted every turn.

Spicy Spray.effect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move, it burns the attacker.

Stall.effect: The Pokémon moves after all other Pokémon do.

Stalwart.effect: Ignores the effects of opposing Pokémon's Abilities and moves that draw in moves.

Stamina.effect: Boosts the Defense stat when hit by an attack.

Stance Change.effect: The Pokémon changes its form to Blade Forme when it uses an attack move, and changes to Shield Forme when it uses King's Shield.

Static.effect: The Pokémon is charged with static electricity, so contact with it may cause paralysis.

Steadfast.effect: The Pokémon's determination boosts the Speed stat each time the Pokémon flinches.

Stench.effect: By releasing stench when attacking, this Pokémon may cause the target to flinch.

Sticky Hold.effect: Items held by the Pokémon are stuck fast and cannot be removed by other Pokémon.

Strong Jaw.effect: The Pokémon's strong jaw boosts the power of its biting moves.

Sturdy.effect: It cannot be knocked out with one hit. One-hit KO moves cannot knock it out, either.

Super Luck.effect: The Pokémon is so lucky that the critical-hit ratios of its moves are boosted.

Supersweet Syrup.effect: A sickly sweet scent spreads across the field the first time the Pokémon enters a battle, lowering the evasiveness of opposing Pokémon.

Supreme Overlord.effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, its Attack and Sp. Atk stats are boosted by 10% for each of the allies in its party that have already been defeated.

Surge Surfer.effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed stat on Electric Terrain.

Swarm.effect: Powers up Bug-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

Sweet Veil.effect: Prevents itself and ally Pokémon from falling asleep.

Swift Swim.effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in rain.

Symbiosis.effect: The Pokémon passes its item to an ally that has used up an item.

Synchronize.effect: The attacker will receive the same status condition if it inflicts a burn, poison, or paralysis to the Pokémon.

Tangled Feet.effect: Raises evasion if the Pokémon is confused.

Technician.effect: Powers up the Pokémon's weaker moves.
Technician.damageCondition: moves with base power 60 or less

Telepathy.effect: Anticipates an ally's attack and dodges it.

Thick Fat.effect: The Pokémon is protected by a layer of thick fat, which halves the damage taken from Fire- and Ice-type moves.

Torrent.effect: Powers up Water-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

Tough Claws.effect: Powers up moves that make direct contact.

Toxic Debris.effect: Scatters poison spikes at the feet of the opposing team when the Pokémon takes damage from physical moves.

Trace.effect: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon copies an opposing Pokémon's Ability.

Unaware.effect: When attacking, the Pokémon ignores the target Pokémon's stat changes.

Unburden.effect: Boosts the Speed stat if the Pokémon's held item is  used or lost.

Unnerve.effect: Unnerves opposing Pokémon and makes them unable to eat Berries.

Unseen Fist.effect: The Pokémon can deal damage with moves that make physical contact, even if the target is protected. This damage is reduced to 25%

Vital Spirit.effect: The Pokémon is full of vitality, and that prevents it from falling asleep.

Volt Absorb.effect: Restores HP if hit by an Electric-type move, instead of taking damage.

Wandering Spirit.effect: The Pokémon exchanges Abilities with a Pokémon that hits it with a move that makes direct contact.

Water Absorb.effect: Restores HP if hit by a Water-type move, instead of taking damage.

Water Bubble.effect: Lowers the power of Fire-type moves done to the Pokémon and prevents the Pokémon from getting a burn.

Weak Armor.effect: Physical attacks to the Pokémon lower its Defense stat but sharply raise its Speed stat.

White Smoke.effect: The Pokémon is protected by its white smoke, which prevents other Pokémon from lowering its stats.

Zero to Hero.effect: The Pokémon transforms into its Hero Form when it switches out.
